Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a curvature-aware precision routing framework can effectively optimize mixed-precision quantization for deep neural networks, leading to improved accuracy and efficiency.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization, but they rely on heuristic-based methods or uniform precision assignment. Our approach differs by incorporating curvature-aware precision routing, which tightly couples geometric subspace diagnostics with deterministic optimization proxies.
Abstract: This paper proposes a curvature-aware precision routing framework for mixed-precision quantization in deep neural networks. Our approach integrates spectral quantization theory with low-overhead implementation, bridging foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework through rigorous evaluation cycles, showcasing improved accuracy and efficiency compared to state-of-the-art methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework on various deep neural network architectures, including ResNet and MobileNet.
Compare the performance of our approach with existing mixed-precision quantization methods, such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT.
Investigate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on different quantization schemes, including post-training quantization and training-based quantization.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the curvature-aware precision routing framework may increase computational overhead.
The effectiveness of our approach may depend on the specific deep neural network architecture and quantization scheme used.
